message:
test(frankenterm-core): freeze agent_inventory_contract.json golden for robot payload (ft-bs0ec)

Pin the canonical JSON serialization of AgentInventoryData so any accidental
rename, re-ordering, or type change in robot_types is caught before it ships
to frontend consumers that key off the payload shape.

## New test
crates/frankenterm-core/tests/agent_inventory_golden.rs — three tests:

1. agent_inventory_contract_matches_golden
   Serializes a mock AgentInventoryData via serde_json::to_value, runs it
   through pretty_canonical (recursive Map-sort + 2-space pretty print), and
   compares against fixtures/agent_inventory_contract.json. On drift, writes
   the actual output next to the golden with an .actual.json suffix and
   points the operator at the UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 regeneration command.

2. agent_inventory_contract_is_deterministic
   Captures three serializations back-to-back and asserts all three are
   identical — cheap insurance against accidental nondeterminism
   (HashMap iteration order, timestamps, etc.) sneaking into the robot
   payload.

3. agent_inventory_running_map_uses_stringified_pane_keys
   Runtime spot-check that the `running` map in the serialized JSON keys by
   stringified pane IDs ("7", "42") — this is the contract frontend
   consumers rely on and it's easy to break by flipping a serde attribute.

## Mock data coverage
- mixed installed rows: present + omitted optional fields (binary_path,
  config_path, display_name, evidence, root_paths, version) across 4
  agents (claude, codex, aider, gemini — last one detected=false to pin
  the not-installed shape)
- running map with two pane IDs (7 with pane_title source, 42 with
  pattern_engine source) — keys stringified per Serialize impl
- summary counts (configured_count, installed_but_idle_count,
  installed_count, running_count)
- feature-availability bit filesystem_detection_available

## Regeneration
    UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test agent_inventory_golden

The test honors UPDATE_GOLDEN both for the initial write (if the golden is
missing) and for re-baselining after an intentional contract change.

## New fixture
crates/frankenterm-core/tests/fixtures/agent_inventory_contract.json — 1.4KB
canonical form, BTreeMap-sorted, 2-space indent, trailing newline.

Tracks ft-bs0ec (robot_types agent inventory golden contract).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,68 @@
+{
+  "filesystem_detection_available": true,
+  "installed": [
+    {
+      "binary_path": "/usr/local/bin/claude",
+      "config_path": "/Users/demo/.claude/settings.json",
+      "detected": true,
+      "display_name": "Claude Code",
+      "evidence": [
+        "found ~/.claude",
+        "binary: /usr/local/bin/claude"
+      ],
+      "root_paths": [
+        "/Users/demo/.claude"
+      ],
+      "slug": "claude",
+      "version": "1.2.3"
+    },
+    {
+      "binary_path": "/usr/local/bin/codex",
+      "detected": true,
+      "display_name": "Codex",
+      "root_paths": [
+        "/Users/demo/.codex"
+      ],
+      "slug": "codex",
+      "version": "0.9.0"
+    },
+    {
+      "config_path": "/Users/demo/.config/aider/aider.conf.yml",
+      "detected": true,
+      "evidence": [
+        "detected via ~/.config/aider"
+      ],
+      "slug": "aider"
+    },
+    {
+      "detected": false,
+      "display_name": "Gemini CLI",
+      "evidence": [
+        "not installed"
+      ],
+      "slug": "gemini"
+    }
+  ],
+  "running": {
+    "42": {
+      "display_name": "Codex",
+      "pane_id": 42,
+      "session_id": "sess-codex-42",
+      "slug": "codex",
+      "source": "pattern_engine",
+      "state": "working"
+    },
+    "7": {
+      "pane_id": 7,
+      "slug": "claude",
+      "source": "pane_title",
+      "state": "waiting_approval"
+    }
+  },
+  "summary": {
+    "configured_count": 2,
+    "installed_but_idle_count": 1,
+    "installed_count": 3,
+    "running_count": 2
+  }
+}
